text:
It芒聙聶s Day 11 of the Olympics, so you probably have your routine down by now. Hopefully you芒聙聶ve been checking out this post every day, which means you know the drill. My daily watch guide is here to help you know what events need to be on your screen(s).

Hopefully you芒聙聶ve also subscribed toSports Illustrated芒聙聶s Daily Rings, my daily podcast where Dan Gartland and I recap as much as we possibly can every single day. New episodes have been published every night before NBC芒聙聶s prime-time coverage is even over. You can also see clips of the podcast on YouTube (likeour segment Monday night about Mondo Duplantis).

Here芒聙聶s what I芒聙聶ll be watching Tuesday, August 6.

This is not a comprehensive list;visit Olympics.comfor a full schedule. All events will stream live on Peacock. I芒聙聶ll also be tweeting out a schedule of medal events, Team USA games and other favorites each day.

My Day 11 Olympics viewing guide! Getting smaller as U.S. teams get knocked out, but the number of medal events will go back up after Tuesday#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/n0srmuykCC

There seems to be another big event every day on the track, and now it芒聙聶s time for the women芒聙聶s 200 meters. Team USA芒聙聶s Gabby Thomas won bronze in Tokyo and is now the favorite, but don芒聙聶t forget about St. Lucia芒聙聶s Julien Alfred, who won the 100 and will now go for the double.

Another race getting a lot of hype is the men芒聙聶s 1500 meters. There is a fun rivalry between the Tokyo Olympic champ, Jakob Ingebrigtsen of Norway, and the reigning world champ, Great Britain芒聙聶s Josh Kerr. This is going to be an entertaining race.

The track finals start at 1:35 p.m. ET and also include medal events in the women芒聙聶s hammer throw, men芒聙聶s long jump and women芒聙聶s 3000-meter steeplechase.脗

Wrestling began Monday, and we芒聙聶ll see the first medals handed out Tuesday. We knew coming into the Olympics that Amit Elor was one of Team USA芒聙聶s best chances at a gold medal in wrestling, and her weight class was up on the first day.

She won her first three matches in dominating fashion and will be in the finals of the women芒聙聶s 68-kg freestyle competition, which starts at 12:15 p.m. ET. But her gold medal match will be the 15th bout on that mat, so it芒聙聶s tough to give an exact start time.

I haven芒聙聶t brought up handball very much in this space, but it芒聙聶s always a fan favorite. They are now out of group play and into the knockout rounds. All four women芒聙聶s quarterfinals will be on Tuesday, then the four men芒聙聶s games will be on Friday.

Games are a full hour and they芒聙聶ll be running throughout the day. So now is a perfect time to pick a team and hop on a bandwagon. Every team still alive is three wins from a gold medal.

Now that sports are transitioning into knockout stages, there is a serious lineup of big games for U.S. teams:

Mitch Goldich is a senior editor for Sports Illustrated, mostly focused on the NFL. He has also covered the Olympics extensively and written on a variety of sports since joining SI in 2014. His work has been published by The New York Times, Baseball Prospectus and Food & Wine, among other outlets. Goldich has a bachelor芒聙聶s in journalism from Lehigh University and a master芒聙聶s in journalism from the Medill School at Northwestern University. He is a Philly native, now living in Boston, and he invented and popularized the octopus, when the same player scores a touchdown and the ensuing two-point conversion.

title: Gabby Thomas芒聙聶s 200-Meter Final Highlights Tuesday芒聙聶s Olympic Events
description: Thomas won bronze in Tokyo and is now the favorite, but she has fierce competition. Plus, many sports including men芒聙聶s basketball, move into the knockout rounds.
